title: Index template and alias configuration
description:
**As a** Plexus runtime, **I want** the OpenSearch domain to have pre-configured index templates and aliases, **so that** the blue/green index rebuild strategy works from the first run.

The stack should include a custom resource or bootstrap script that creates an index template defining the mapping for topic memory documents: `doc_id` (keyword), `text` (text), `embedding` (knn_vector, 384 dims), `metadata` (object), `cluster_id` (keyword), `cluster_version` (keyword), `record_type` (keyword for item vs cluster discrimination).

It also creates index aliases `topic-memory-live` and `topic-memory-build` for zero-downtime blue/green deployment.

## Key Files (infra repo)

- Modify: `opensearch_infra/stacks/opensearch_stack.py`
- Modify: `tests/test_opensearch_stack.py`

## Dependencies

Story plx-a2a3cb (CDK stack synthesizes a valid OpenSearch domain)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: Index template and alias configuration

  Scenario: Index template defines knn_vector field for embeddings
    Given the index template configuration
    When the mappings are inspected
    Then there is a field "embedding" of type "knn_vector" with dimension 384
    And there is a field "text" of type "text"
    And there is a field "doc_id" of type "keyword"
    And there is a field "cluster_id" of type "keyword"
    And there is a field "record_type" of type "keyword"
    And there is a field "metadata" of type "object"

  Scenario: Blue/green aliases are defined
    Given the index template configuration
    When the aliases section is inspected
    Then aliases "topic-memory-live" and "topic-memory-build" are defined
    And the template pattern matches "topic-memory-*"

  Scenario: Alias names are in stack outputs
    Given the synthesized CDK template
    When outputs are inspected
    Then there is an Output named "LiveIndexAlias" with value "topic-memory-live"
    And there is an Output named "BuildIndexAlias" with value "topic-memory-build"
